siteSwitcher.label: Site
siteSwitcher.notFound: That site does not exist, or you do not have access to it.

dashboard.noSiteDescription: Your account is not assigned to a site. Ask an administrator for access.
dashboard.noTypes: This site has not declared any content types yet.

marketplace.title: Marketplace review
marketplace.description: Decide what the marketplace is allowed to serve, and pull a version out of circulation when it turns out it should not have been served.
marketplace.denied: You do not have permission to review marketplace packages.

queue.heading: Review queue
queue.description: Packages waiting for a decision. Read the scanner findings before you approve: an approval is what lets this code run on live sites.
queue.emptyDescription: Every submitted package has been decided on.
queue.noFindingsDescription: A clean scan is not a review. The scanner only catches what it has rules for — read the code before approving it.

decision.approveBody: Approving publishes this version. Sites will be able to install it, and its code will run inside their runtimes.
decision.rejectBody: Rejecting keeps this version out of the marketplace. The publisher can submit a new version once the findings are addressed.
decision.blockingWarning: The scanner raised {count} blocking finding(s). Approving overrides it. Only do that if you have read the code and know why the rule does not apply.
decision.noteHint: Optional. Goes to the publisher with your decision — say what convinced you, or what has to change.

published.heading: Published versions
published.description: Everything the marketplace serves today, and what each version is running on.
published.emptyDescription: Approved packages appear here once they are published.

revoke.title: Revoke {name} v{version}?
revoke.runningWarning: This is not a takedown of something waiting to be installed. This version is already installed and running — revoking pulls code that live sites are executing right now.
revoke.blastRadius: {count} live site(s) will be moved off this version.
revoke.blastRadiusNone: No site is running this version at the moment. Revoking still prevents any new install of it.
revoke.effectsHeading: What happens the moment you confirm
revoke.effectTheme: Every site on this theme falls back to the built-in default theme. Their pages keep rendering, but they will not look the same.
revoke.effectPlugin: The plugin is quarantined on every site that installed it. It stops handling hooks; the site keeps running without it.
revoke.effectCaches: The theme and plugin runtime caches are purged, so the revoked code stops executing immediately rather than at the next deploy.
revoke.irreversible: This cannot be undone. There is no un-revoke: if the package is later cleared, it has to be republished as a new version.
revoke.reasonHint: Required. Recorded against the version and shown to the publisher — the next person to look at this will have only what you write here.
revoke.reasonPlaceholder: e.g. Reads process.env and posts the values to an external host during setup()

actions.reviewDenied: You do not have permission to review marketplace packages.
actions.reviewFailed: The decision could not be recorded.
actions.revoked: {name} v{version} revoked. {count} site(s) were moved off this version.
actions.revokeFailed: The version could not be revoked.
actions.reasonRequired: A reason is required to revoke a version.

browse.title: Marketplace
browse.description: Browse themes and plugins and install them into this site.
browse.denied: You do not have permission to browse the marketplace.
browse.emptyDescription: No packages have been published to the marketplace this instance points at.
browse.installFailed: The package could not be installed.
browse.installDenied: You do not have permission to install this.

sync.local: This instance is its own marketplace — packages are installed from its own catalogue.
sync.fresh: Shopping at {url}. Revocation feed current as of {synced} — {count} package(s) revoked.
sync.staleBody: This instance has not heard from {url} since {synced}. Installed packages keep running (revocation is fail-open), but a package pulled by the marketplace since then would still be executing here. Check that the worker and the marketplace are reachable.

jobs.title: Background jobs
jobs.description: The dead-letter queue: jobs that failed every attempt and were parked here. Nothing will run them again unless you say so.
jobs.denied: You do not have permission to manage background jobs.
jobs.emptyDescription: Every background job has run to completion.

discardDialog.title: Discard {name}?
discardDialog.body: Discarding deletes the job. The work it was going to do will never be done — it is not retried, and nothing is left behind except what you already see here. If it was going to send an email, the email is never sent.
discardDialog.retryFirst: If there is any chance the failure was transient, retry it instead. A retry that fails again lands back on this list; a discard does not come back.

actions.denied: You do not have permission to manage background jobs.
actions.retryFailed: The job could not be retried.
actions.discardFailed: The job could not be discarded.

jobs.truncated: Showing {shown} of {total} failed jobs. Retrying everything on this page will not empty the queue.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: This content, or this content type, does not exist on the current site.

users.title: Users
users.description: Who has access to this tenant, and as what. A role can be granted on one site or across every site.
users.noRoles: No role — cannot sign in to anything
users.emptyTitle: No one else has access yet
users.emptyDescription: Create a user to give them a role on this site or across the tenant.

role.title: Set a role for {name}
role.body: Granting a role on a site replaces any role they already hold there. Granting one across the tenant applies it to every site.
role.scopeHint: A tenant-wide role covers every site, including ones created later.

removeDialog.title: Remove {name}?
removeDialog.body: They lose access immediately and every session of theirs is signed out. Their pages, uploads and audit trail stay exactly where they are — this removes the person, not their work.

removeRoleDialog.title: Take away {role} on {scope}?
removeRoleDialog.body: They keep their account and any other roles. They are signed out, so the change takes effect at once.
removeRoleDialog.success: The role has been taken away.
removeRoleDialog.failed: Could not take away the role.

invite.heading: Create user
invite.description: The account can sign in immediately. Email is only a notification with the login details.
invite.name: Name
invite.createdBodyQueued: The account is active now. A notification email with these login details has been queued.
invite.createdBodyManual: The account is active now, but email was not queued. Copy these login details and send them securely.
invite.linkBody: This is the only time it will be shown. Only its hash is stored, so it cannot be looked up again — if it is lost, withdraw the invitation and send a new one. It works once and expires in 7 days.

users.denied: You do not have permission to manage users.

resetTwoFactorDialog.title: Reset two-factor for {name}?
resetTwoFactorDialog.body: For the colleague whose phone is gone and whose recovery codes are somewhere they can no longer reach. They keep their account, their role and their password, and are signed out so they will see the change at once.
resetTwoFactorDialog.warning: Until they enroll again, their account is defended by a password alone — the exact situation two-factor exists to survive. Only do this when you are sure who you are talking to.
resetTwoFactorDialog.success: Two-factor has been reset for {name}. Ask them to enroll again.

profile.title: Your profile
profile.description: Your name, and the password you sign in with.

details.heading: Details
details.emailHint: Your email is your sign-in name and cannot be changed here.
details.name: Name
details.avatarUrlHint: Optional. A link to an image — upload one to the media library and paste its URL.

password.heading: Change password
password.description: Changing your password signs you out of every device, this one included. That is the point: the usual reason to change it is that someone else may have it.
password.mismatch: The two passwords do not match.

roles.heading: Your access
roles.description: The roles you hold, and where.

twoFactor.heading: Two-factor authentication
twoFactor.enabledBody: Signing in takes your password and a code from your authenticator. A password on its own — leaked, phished, or reused from somewhere else — is no longer enough to get in.
twoFactor.disabledBody: Your account is defended by a password alone. Add an authenticator app and a leaked password stops being enough to sign in as you.
twoFactor.disabled: Two-factor is off. Your account is back to a password alone.
twoFactor.regenerateFailed: Could not issue new recovery codes.

setup.title: Set up your authenticator
setup.body: Scan this with Google Authenticator, 1Password, or any other TOTP app. Then type the six digits it shows, so we both know it worked.
setup.qrAlt: QR code for your authenticator app
setup.manual: Cannot scan? Type this into the app instead:
setup.codeHint: Two-factor is not on until this code checks out — a QR you never scanned cannot lock you out.

disableDialog.title: Turn off two-factor?
disableDialog.body: Takes your password AND a code. Either one on its own would be enough to strip the very protection that exists because the other can be stolen.

regenerateDialog.title: Issue new recovery codes?
regenerateDialog.body: The old codes stop working the moment the new ones exist. If you have them written down somewhere, that copy becomes worthless.

codes.heading: Your recovery codes
codes.body: This is the only time they are shown — only their hashes are stored, so there is no way to see them again. Each works once, and they are what gets you in when your phone does not. Save them somewhere that is not your phone.

sites.title: Sites
sites.subtitle: Every site in your tenant. A site serves nothing until it is published.
sites.created: Site created. It is a draft — publish it when it is ready.
sites.empty: No sites yet. Create the first one below.
sites.name: Name
sites.slugHelp: Lowercase letters, digits and hyphens. Unique within your tenant.
sites.hostnameHelp: The address visitors use, port included in development — e.g. localhost:3100. Unique across the platform.
sites.hostnameInvalid: That is not an address we can serve. Use a hostname such as example.com, or localhost:3100 in development.
sites.publishNowHelp: {hostname} starts answering requests as soon as the site is created.
sites.draftHelp: The site will be created as a draft. Drafts are not served — its address will return 404 until you publish it.
sites.brandHelp: Your site's colour and logo. Every theme reads these, so they survive a theme change — a theme may still override them in its own settings.
sites.logoHelp: Shown by the theme wherever it draws your logo. A transparent PNG or SVG reads best.
sites.draftNotice: This site is a draft: it answers no requests until you publish it.

errors.createDenied: You do not have permission to create a site.
errors.updateDenied: You do not have permission to change this site.
